metadata.title: Why adding one capability at a time changes what the same agent produces
metadata.purpose: Each SDK primitive unlocks a distinct behavior class — invention → retrieval → parallel delegation → cross-session context — so the output jumps categories rather than sliding up a quality scale.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: c2v (task prompt → four stage outputs, hallucinate→fetch→delegate→recall), raster (snippet per stage)
metadata.prerequisites: what an agent is, what tools/sub-agents are
metadata.exclusions: SDK wiring code, single-stage plumbing, how to author a tool or sub-agent
metadata.visual_object: A four-panel stack of the SAME briefing output, mutating panel to panel.
metadata.hook: One agent, one briefing task, four runs; each run flips a single boolean, and the same prompt produces a qualitatively different answer.

beats.narration_text: "Brief three companies." Stage 0 (chat only) confidently invents growth figures; Stage 1 (tools on) looks the numbers up; Stage 2 (sub-agents on) spawns one researcher per company in parallel; Stage 3 (memory on) opens with "last time you flagged the profitability gap."
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Adding a primitive should make an agent incrementally better; here each primitive flips it into a different KIND of behavior — why does capability change behavior in category, not degree?
One agent, one briefing task, four runs; each run flips a single boolean, and the same prompt produces a qualitatively different answer.
Each SDK primitive unlocks a distinct behavior class — invention → retrieval → parallel delegation → cross-session context — so the output jumps categories rather than sliding up a quality scale.
beats.visual: A four-panel stack of the SAME briefing output, mutating panel to panel.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Five companies. Stage 0 hallucinates "+40%" (all wrong); Stage 1 fetches real figures (slower, right); Stage 2 runs five researchers at once; Stage 3 leads with a recalled note about Tinplate.
Each SDK primitive unlocks a distinct behavior class — invention → retrieval → parallel delegation → cross-session context — so the output jumps categories rather than sliding up a quality scale.
